data.title: Sintana Energy Inc. (TSX-V: SEI, AIM: SEI, OTCQX: SEUSF) announces admission to trading on AIM in London market.
data.description: Sintana Energy Inc. has recently shared the news of its common shares (TSX-V: SEI, AIM: SEI, OTCQX: SEUSF) being admitted for trading on the AIM market of the London Stock Exchange plc. This move marks a significant step for the company, allowing its Common Shares to be traded on AIM. The development is expected to enhance visibility and accessibility for investors interested in Sintana, demonstrating the company's growth and potential in the market. This strategic decision aligns with the company's objectives for expansion and increasing shareholder value.

data.title: Silver breaks above $70, Novo Nordisk surges 9% on obesity pill approval. Wall Street rises, S&P 500 at 6,900, with 4.3% US GDP growth in Q3. Fed easing expectations decrease to 15%.
data.description: On Wednesday, Wall Street saw modest gains as investors anticipated a strong year-end period. By midday in New York, the S&P 500 rose to around 6,900, nearing October’s record of 6,920. The Nasdaq 100 also increased by 0.4% while the Dow Jones Industrial Average went up by 0.3%. However, small caps did not perform as well, with the Russell 2000 down by approximately 0.6%. The day was influenced by the release of better-than-expected U.S. GDP data. In the third quarter, the GDP grew at a 4.3% annualized rate, surpassing the estimated 3.3% and being the quickest growth in two years. As a result, traders adjusted their expectations for Federal Reserve easing. There is now a 15% likelihood of a 25-basis-point cut in late January, reduced from the previous day’s 20%.

data.title: Wilmington's new TV series Coastal Connection added to REAL Shows Network, focusing on real estate and lifestyle in the city.
data.description: In Wilmington, North Carolina, on December 23, 2025, REAL Shows Network (RSN), a national TV channel focused on real estate and lifestyle narratives, introduced Coastal Connection as part of its programming lineup. The show, hosted by a prominent figure in the local real estate sector, aims to showcase the unique lifestyle and property offerings in the region.

data.title: Analyst trims Meta stock price target, expects wounded sentiment until 2026. Will it recover? #Investors #Meta #Stocks
data.description: Analysts at Baird recommended buying Meta stock, although they anticipate continued negative sentiment for the social media company through 2026. They have reduced the price target for Meta stock. The possibility of a recovery in sentiment is questioned for the year 2026.

data.title: Halozyme Therapeutics stock shows strength, upgraded IBD Relative Strength Rating due to improving price performance.
data.description: Halozyme Therapeutics has demonstrated positive improvement in its price performance, leading to an upgrade in its IBD Relative Strength Rating. This upgrade reflects the company's strengthening position in the market. This enhancement in rating signifies the potential for increased growth and stability in Halozyme Therapeutics' stock.

data.title: Applied Optoelectronics stock sees price performance increase, earns upgrade to IBD Relative Strength Rating.
data.description: Applied Optoelectronics has demonstrated an increase in price performance, resulting in an upgrade to its IBD Relative Strength Rating. This upgrade showcases the company's strong performance in the market.

data.title: Pfizer discloses patient death in marstacimab study for hemophilia. FDA approved Hympavzi for hemophilia in October 2024.
data.description: Pfizer Inc. (PFE) announced the tragic death of a participant in a long-term study of marstacimab for hemophilia A or B. The FDA had approved Hympavzi (marstacimab-hncq) in October 2024 for prophylaxis in hemophilia patients. The European Hemophilia Consortium shared this update. The patient passed away on December 14, 2025, due to severe adverse events, namely cerebellar infarction and cerebral hemorrhage. The individual had hemophilia A with inhibitors. For more information, you can find the full story on Benzinga.com. Despite pipeline efforts and cost reductions, Pfizer forecasts slower growth in 2026.

data.title: Marissa Mayer's start-up Dazzle AI raises $8M in seed funding with a $35M valuation, led by Kirsten Green.
data.description: Dazzle AI, a recently established company led by entrepreneur Marissa Mayer, has revealed securing $8 million in seed funding, reaching a post-money valuation of $35 million. Forerunner's Kirsten Green spearheaded the funding round, supported by Kleiner Perkins, Greycroft, Offline Ventures, Slow Ventures, and Bling Capital. Leveraging her background at Google and Yahoo, Mayer is committed to advancing Dazzle AI's mission.

data.title: 1st Security Bank appoints Omeed Salashoor as Senior VP, Director of Home Lending.
data.description: In Mountlake Terrace, Washington on December 23, 2025, FS Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ: FSBW), the parent company of 1st Security Bank of Washington, revealed the selection of Omeed Salashoor as the Senior Vice President and Director of Home Lending for 1st Security Bank of Washington.

data.title: Bitcoin's $150,000 dreams dead due to volatility compression, no more 80% crashes; BTC dropped from $126,000 to $87,000.
data.description: Bitcoin's volatility compression has dashed hopes of a $150,000 surge, with the cryptocurrency dropping from a high of $126,000 to $87,000. This shift in volatility means less explosive price movements, reducing the potential for massive crashes. Professional Capital Management's Anthony Pompliano highlighted this change, noting that a 40% drawdown is now more likely compared to the previous 80% drops. While this means sacrificing euphoric rallies, it also mitigates significant losses, potentially leading to a more stable market. Despite recent fluctuations, Bitcoin is still up 100% over the past two years.

data.title: Dow gains 100 points as US durable goods orders decline by 2.2% in October to $307.4 billion, missing market estimates.
data.description: During midday trading, U.S. stocks were on the rise, with the Dow Jones index increasing by more than 100 points on Tuesday. The Dow climbed 0.22% to 48,469.41, while the NASDAQ went up by 0.40% to reach 23,521.69. The S&P 500 also experienced gains, rising by 0.34% to 6,901.90. Communication services sector shares saw a 0.5% increase, while consumer staples stocks dropped by 0.5% on Tuesday. U.S. durable goods orders fell by 2.2% month-over-month to $307.4 billion in October, contrasting a revised 0.7% growth from September, missing market expectations of a 1.5% decline. Noteworthy stock movements include Novo Nordisk A/S (NYSE: NVO) which surged 9% to $52.30 following FDA approval of its Wegovy pill, and Highway Holdings Ltd (NASDAQ: HIHO) which also saw an increase. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Royce Micro-Cap Trust (RMT) saw average weekly trading volume of 556,750 shares on Nov 30, 2025. NAV $11.86, MKT $10.46, with an average annual total return.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, it was reported that the average weekly trading volume was around 556,750 shares. The fund's adviser boasts over five decades of experience in small- and micro-cap investments. As of November 30, 2025, the closing prices were noted as NAV at 11.86 and MKT at 10.46. The average annual total return as of the same date in terms of NAV was mentioned but specific percentage data was not provided.

data.title: BioMarin Pharma acquires Amicus Therapeutics for $4.8B, accelerating revenue growth. Analysts support the deal due to strategic fit and revenue diversification.
data.description: BioMarin Pharmaceutical Inc. has agreed to acquire Amicus Therapeutics, Inc. for $4.8 billion, aiming to boost revenue growth and financial outlook. The acquisition is expected to be accretive to Non-GAAP Diluted EPS within the first year. Truist Securities maintains BioMarin with a Buy rating, raising price forecast to $100. Analysts view the acquisition positively, noting the addition of rare disease therapies to BioMarin's portfolio, with clear synergies and no clinical development risk. The deal diversifies revenue base and alleviates potential revenue pressure, enhancing BioMarin's position in the market.

data.title: Identis Group invests in Credence ID, merging physical and digital ID tech. Expands presence in high-growth markets.
data.description: Identis Group, a prominent player in Secure Identity and Payment technologies worldwide, has recently made a significant move by investing in Credence ID, a top contender in mobile identity solutions, biometrics, and standardized digital ID offerings. This strategic investment marks a crucial point in Identis' expansion plans, allowing the Group to broaden its reach in rapidly growing markets and bolster its international presence. The integration of Credence ID into Identis' ecosystem marks a completion of its initiative to enhance its offerings and seize new opportunities in the industry.
